subagent_reports.topic: Validate /adv-coordinate as read-first ADV command (doc+manifest+docs/tests), not a new MCP tool or CLI mutation verb

sources.label: COMMAND_MANIFEST shape + non-gate exemplars (adv-cleanup, adv-triage, adv-epic)
sources.summary: CommandDef requires name/description/phase/requiresChangeId/prerequisites/successors. scope/gate/phaseGoal/args_hint optional. adv-cleanup uses phase:'advanced', adv-triage/adv-epic use utility/pre-impl with no gate, no phaseGoal. /adv-coordinate fits this non-gate, non-workflow shape exactly. Existing 27th index lives here.

sources.label: manifest.test.ts hardcoded list + count + voice rules
sources.summary: expectedCommands[] array and toHaveLength(27) must update to 28 and add 'adv-coordinate'. Description must start with strong verb (allowlist L188-210 — 'Coordinate' is NOT in current STRONG_VERBS list), be 5-14 words, no banned phrases. Non-workflow command MUST NOT have phaseGoal (L421-433).

sources.label: manifest-doc-drift.test.ts: doc+README+ADV_INSTRUCTIONS table drift
sources.summary: Enforces: every manifest cmd has a doc; every doc has manifest entry; frontmatter description EXACTLY equals manifest; README.md AND ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md command tables contain every manifest command with exact-match description. Single-line YAML scalar only. New command auto-pulled into all four checks.

sources.label: docs/command-voice-standard.md voice canon
sources.summary: manifest.ts is single source of truth; descriptions verb-first, 5-14 words, mention primary output/gate effect, no undefined jargon. Enforcement tests named. Banned-phrase + verb-first asserted in manifest.test.ts.

sources.label: adv-cleanup.md / adv-triage.md read-first + dry-run-by-default + Tier B approval pattern
sources.summary: Canonical reference architecture for the proposed command: Markdown prompt; frontmatter name+description; manifest comment block; scan/categorize/present-findings phases; mutations only after explicit Tier B inline whitelist approval (rq-inlineApproval01.4); 'Runs inline; no sub-agents'; Coexistence + Key Tools tables. adv-coordinate should mirror this exactly.

sources.label: adv-epic.md goal-first Epic command + Command Boundary block
sources.summary: Command Boundary 'Produces/MUST NOT/Gate' pattern. Explicit MUST NOT: add CLI mutation verbs, make membership mandatory, treat Epic order as blocking, add Jira-like assignees/estimates/sprints/boards. Mutations route through typed Epic tools only (adv_epic_update/reorder/repair_membership). adv-coordinate must reuse these exact typed tools, not add new ones.

sources.label: advance-epics spec (law): advisory order, optional membership, typed audited mutation, no Jira clone
sources.locator: adv_spec advance-epics rq-epicOrderAdvisory01 / rq-epicOptionalMembership01 / rq-epicMembershipRepair01 / rq-epicNoJiraClone01 / rq-epicMutableScope01
sources.summary: Order MUST be advisory (display+next-work only, never hard-block). Membership MUST be optional. Link/unlink/move/repair MUST require audit evidence + dryRun-capable + typed status (projection_pending/stale/target_unreachable). update/reorder require expected_version (optimistic concurrency). rq-epicNoJiraClone01: v1 MUST NOT add assignments/estimates/boards/sprints/ownership workflows — 'Epic ordering, next-work recommendation, and compact context are the only planning primitives added in v1.' A coordination/audit READ + approval-gated typed-mutation command is consistent IF it adds no new primitive and runs every mutation through existing audited tools.

sources.label: advance-epics-assets.test.ts contract-asset enforcement
sources.summary: Epic spec/doc/ADR contract tests assert spec doc forbids project-management field clones and states membership optional + order advisory. A coordinate command that 'hard-gates on Epic order' or 'mandates membership' would contradict these — proposal correctly lists those as out-of-scope.

sources.label: OpenCode official commands doc — directory convention discrepancy
sources.summary: Official docs: markdown command files live under '.opencode/commands/' (PLURAL) or via config 'command'. Frontmatter supports description/agent/model/subtask; $ARGUMENTS + $1.. + !`cmd` + @file. THIS REPO uses '.opencode/command/' (SINGULAR) consistently and deploy-local.sh syncs it — follow the repo's established singular convention; do not introduce plural.

sources.label: Drift surfaces beyond proposal scope (4 extra files)
sources.locator: README.md L401/419-420; ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md L35,124,158-159; SETUP.md L1343,1363; docs/cli-surface-matrix.md L27-37
sources.summary: Proposal lists README + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS. Discovered 3 MORE surfaces carrying per-command rows: (1) ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md L35 collaboration-mode table (separate from L124 command table), (2) SETUP.md command table (L1343/1363 — NOT drift-tested but user-facing), (3) docs/cli-surface-matrix.md classifies each /adv-* as 'agent-workflow-only' vs CLI. adv-coordinate should land 'agent-workflow-only' there to assert it adds no CLI mutation verb.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: SOUND. The proposal is a textbook boring-technology fit. /adv-coordinate as (a) a Markdown command prompt under .opencode/command/, (b) a CommandDef registered in COMMAND_MANIFEST, (c) drift-doc + count-test updates, and (d) consistency tests is the exact, proven pattern already used by adv-cleanup, adv-triage, and adv-epic. No new MCP tool, Zod schema, Temporal signal, or CLI verb is needed: every durable Epic mutation already has a typed, audited, dryRun-capable, optimistic-concurrency tool (adv_epic_list/show/update/reorder/repair_membership/move_change/unlink_change). The command is a read-first orchestration prompt over those tools — strictly simpler than any alternative that introduces a coordination engine, a new aggregate tool, or a CLI mutation verb. Reference 'by-the-book' architecture for this stack is precisely: manifest entry + markdown doc + drift tests, mutations via existing typed tools with Tier B inline approval and dry-run default (mirroring adv-cleanup). Deviation from that reference: NONE in mechanism. Spec-law alignment: the coordination workflow stays inside advance-epics avoidances as long as it (1) keeps Epic order ADVISORY (report inversions, never hard-block — rq-epicOrderAdvisory01), (2) keeps membership OPTIONAL (rq-epicOptionalMembership01), (3) routes mutations through audited typed tools with expected_version + evidence (rq-epicMembershipRepair01/rq-epicMutableScope01), and (4) adds NO new planning primitive beyond ordering/next-work/compact-context (rq-epicNoJiraClone01). All four are already in the proposal's out-of-scope list, so the design is self-consistent with the law.

validation.notes: VALIDATED with required corrections (non-blocking but must-do for green tests). 1) manifest.test.ts: bump toHaveLength(27)->28 and add 'adv-coordinate' to expectedCommands[]; this is hardcoded, not derived. 2) Voice gate: 'Coordinate' is NOT in STRONG_VERBS allowlist (manifest.test.ts L188-210) — either start the description with an existing strong verb (e.g. 'Audit', 'Analyze', 'Review', 'Scan', 'Triage') OR add 'Coordinate' to the allowlist in the same change; a description literally starting with 'Coordinate' fails today. 3) Keep manifest description 5-14 words, verb-first, no banned phrases, mention primary output. 4) Non-workflow command: do NOT set phaseGoal (manifest.test.ts L421-433 fails if present). 5) Frontmatter description must be a single-line YAML scalar that EXACTLY equals manifest. 6) Update BOTH README.md and ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md command tables (drift-tested) AND consider SETUP.md L~1363, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md L35 collaboration-mode table, and docs/cli-surface-matrix.md (classify 'agent-workflow-only'). 7) Spec coverage: existing advance-epics spec covers Epic data-model + advisory order + audited mutation but has NO requirement for a coordination/audit *workflow command* analogous to rq-epicCreateCommand01. A MINOR spec delta (e.g. rq-epicCoordinateCommand01: read-first inventory, advisory findings, mutations only via audited typed tools after explicit approval, no new primitive) is the by-the-book way to make this command's contract law and is the cleanest place to encode the out-of-scope avoidances; recommend adding it during design rather than relying on prose alone.

subagent_reports.recommendation: PROCEED to discovery/design with these concrete corrections. (A) Model the command file on adv-cleanup.md: dry-run/report-first default, Command Boundary block ('Produces / × MUST NOT / Gate: None'), explicit MUST NOT list (new Epic mutation tools, mandatory membership, auto-enrollment, hard-gating on Epic order, CLI mutation verbs), Tier B inline whitelist approval before any durable Epic mutation, 'Runs inline; no sub-agents'. (B) Reuse ONLY existing typed Epic tools for mutation (adv_epic_update/reorder/repair_membership/move_change/unlink_change) — all already require expected_version/audit evidence and support dryRun; do NOT add a new aggregate or coordinate tool. (C) Fix the voice gate first: pick a description verb that is in STRONG_VERBS (Audit/Analyze/Review/Triage/Scan) or extend the allowlist; e.g. 'Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health'. (D) Update manifest count 27->28 + expectedCommands[] + all four+ doc surfaces. (E) Add a MINOR advance-epics spec delta (rq-epicCoordinateCommand01) capturing the read-first + advisory-order + audited-mutation + no-new-primitive contract so the avoidances are law, not just prose, and write the asset test to assert it (pattern: advance-epics-assets.test.ts). This keeps correctness structural (P33) rather than prose-only. No anti-patterns detected; this is the simplest viable architecture.
follow_ups: Confirm during design whether a spec delta lands in advance-epics (recommended) vs a separate workflow spec; advance-epics is the natural home given the command operates entirely on Epic state.
Verify deploy-local.sh / overlay sync picks up a new .opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md automatically (it globs adv-*.md) — likely no script change needed, but confirm in design.
Decide the exact strong verb for the manifest description before writing tests, since the verb-first gate and 5-14 word gate are both hard-failing.
Decide whether SETUP.md and docs/cli-surface-matrix.md rows are in-scope for this change (not drift-tested, but user-facing/governance surfaces — recommend including to avoid stale docs).

subagent_reports.topic: Design-mode leverage scout for /adv-coordinate command addition: contract/test design and command-suite consistency

sources.label: manifest-doc-drift.test.ts (CI-enforced)
sources.summary: Already enforces (a) every manifest command has a .opencode/command/*.md doc, (b) frontmatter description == manifest description exactly, (c) README.md + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md command tables contain every manifest command with matching description. README/ADV_INSTRUCTIONS doc-sync is NOT optional polish — it is an existing hard gate the moment the manifest entry lands.

sources.label: cli-surface-matrix.test.ts (CI-enforced, OMITTED from draft verification)
sources.summary: Iterates every .opencode/command/adv-*.md and fails if no matrix row with a valid command disposition exists. Adding adv-coordinate.md WITHOUT a docs/cli-surface-matrix.md row breaks CI. Draft verification command does NOT include this test file — a real verification gap.

sources.label: advance-epics-assets.test.ts hardcoded req-ID list
sources.summary: Spec-mirror test iterates a HARDCODED req-ID array against docs/specs/advance-epics.md, and separately asserts specific req IDs exist in .adv/specs/advance-epics/spec.json. New rq-epicCoordinateCommand01 will only be covered if explicitly added to both spec sources AND a test assertion. Existing /adv-epic contract tests (lines 72-126) are the exact reusable template for the new command-contract test block.

sources.label: manifest.test.ts command count + voice gates
sources.summary: Hardcodes 'contains all 27 ADV commands' + toHaveLength(27) + expectedCommands array; must become 28 with adv-coordinate added. Voice tests already enforce: strong-verb start (allowlist includes 'Audit' implicitly? NO — see risk), 5-14 words, no banned phrases. Draft description 'Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health' must pass these.

sources.label: STRONG_VERBS allowlist (voice gate)
sources.summary: STRONG_VERBS array does NOT currently contain 'Audit'. Draft description starts with 'Audit'. The 'every description starts with a strong verb' test (line 212) will FAIL unless 'Audit' is added to STRONG_VERBS or the description verb is changed to an existing one (e.g. 'Analyze', 'Scan', 'Review', 'Detect').

sources.label: advance-epics spec.json version + structure
sources.summary: Spec is at 1.4.0. Adding rq-epicCoordinateCommand01 requires a version bump and a typed requirement object with scenarios, mirrored as prose in docs/specs/advance-epics.md. rq-epicCliList01.4 already establishes the 'CLI namespace remains read-only / no mutation verbs' precedent the new req can cite for the no-CLI-mutation avoidance.

sources.label: adv-cleanup.md approval-gate template
sources.summary: adv-cleanup is the canonical read-first / dry-run-default / per-bucket Tier B approval / atomic-apply template. Its Phase 1 scan -> Phase 2 categorize -> Phase 3 report -> Phase 4 approval -> Phase 5 apply structure maps 1:1 onto coordinate's inventory->alignment->sequencing->health->report->approval-gated-actions flow. Reuse verbatim structure; only swap adv_change_* tools for adv_epic_* tools.

sources.label: Available typed Epic tools (reuse mandate)
sources.locator: tool schema: adv_epic_list, adv_epic_show, adv_epic_update (expected_version), adv_epic_reorder (expected_version), adv_epic_repair_membership, adv_epic_move_change, adv_epic_link_change
sources.summary: adv_epic_show returns version for optimistic concurrency; adv_epic_update + adv_epic_reorder both REQUIRE expected_version. The 'current expected_version' constraint is structurally enforced by the tools themselves — command just must read-then-mutate. adv_epic_repair_membership supports dryRun + evidence. No new tools needed.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The change is a pure asset/contract/doc addition with zero runtime code — leverage comes entirely from reusing existing enforced harnesses rather than writing new ones. The dominant risk is UNDER-counting the CI gates the new command file trips, not design complexity. Four CI-enforced harnesses react automatically to a new command/spec: manifest.test.ts (count 27->28 + voice), manifest-doc-drift.test.ts (frontmatter + README + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS tables), cli-surface-matrix.test.ts (matrix row), advance-epics-assets.test.ts (spec mirror). The draft design's verification command covers only 3 of the relevant test files and OMITS cli-surface-matrix.test.ts, and the draft description 'Audit ...' trips the STRONG_VERBS allowlist which lacks 'Audit'. adv-cleanup.md is a near-perfect approval-gate structural template, and the typed Epic tools already enforce expected_version + dryRun + evidence, so the 'no direct state edit / current expected_version / audit evidence' constraints are largely structural-by-reuse rather than requiring new prose enforcement.

validation.notes: No architectural blocker. Two verification gaps and one voice-gate collision must be closed in design before apply, or apply will hit red CI: (1) add cli-surface-matrix row + test file to verification; (2) resolve 'Audit' verb vs STRONG_VERBS allowlist; (3) wire rq-epicCoordinateCommand01 into BOTH spec sources + an explicit assets-test assertion (the hardcoded req-ID list will not auto-cover it).

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt 5 leverage/scope-risk fixes before independent design validation: (1) EXPAND verification command to include src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts and add the docs/cli-surface-matrix.md row (disposition agent-workflow-only, mirroring /adv-cleanup and /adv-epic rows) — this is a hard CI gate the draft omitted. (2) RESOLVE the description verb: either add 'Audit' to STRONG_VERBS in manifest.test.ts, or change the verb to an existing allowlisted one (recommend 'Audit' addition since AC text says 'Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health' — but flag this as a tiny test-harness edit that must accompany the manifest entry). (3) REUSE adv-cleanup.md verbatim as the phase skeleton (scan->categorize->report->Tier-B-approval->atomic-apply) swapping adv_change_* for adv_epic_*; do not invent a new approval pattern. (4) LEAN on tool-enforced expected_version/dryRun/evidence rather than writing new prose guards — the 'current expected_version' + 'audit evidence' constraints are already structural in adv_epic_update/reorder/repair_membership. (5) ADD rq-epicCoordinateCommand01 to BOTH .adv/specs/advance-epics/spec.json (bump version 1.4.0->1.5.0) and the docs/specs/advance-epics.md mirror, AND add an explicit assets-test assertion — the existing hardcoded req-ID array (advance-epics-assets.test.ts:31-44) will NOT auto-validate the new req.
follow_ups: TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION packet anchors were not provided in standard block form; proceeded with embedded TASK + prompt scope. No identity anchors inferred.

Verify whether adv-coordinate should appear in any command's manifest successors/prerequisites arrays (manifest.test.ts:115-133 validates successor/prereq names resolve) — draft leaves it islanded (prereq []/successors []), which is valid but worth a conscious design decision.

Confirm whether README.md groups commands by phase; adv-coordinate placed in phase 'advanced' should be slotted alongside /adv-cleanup and /adv-refactor rows in every doc table for consistency, not appended at end.

sources.label: advance-epics spec (capability law)
sources.summary: rq-epicOrderAdvisory01 (order advisory, must not hard-block), rq-epicOptionalMembership01 (membership optional), rq-epicCreateCommand01 (typed tools not direct state edits), rq-epicMembershipRepair01/.3 (audit evidence + dry-run repair, target_unreachable), rq-epicMutableScope01 (expected_version optimistic concurrency). Design honors all; rq-epicCoordinateCommand01 is a consistent additive requirement.

sources.label: manifest.test.ts strong-verb + count + description-length gates
sources.summary: Line 64 asserts COMMAND_MANIFEST length 27 today (design's 28 = 27+1 is correct). STRONG_VERBS allowlist (lines 188-210) does NOT include 'Audit' — design D2 correctly requires adding it. Description rule 5-14 words: chosen description is 8 words, passes.

sources.label: docs/cli-surface-matrix.md command matrix + test enforcement
sources.summary: File header states cli-surface-matrix.test.ts fails CI on command additions without a matching row. adv-cleanup (line 28) and adv-epic (line 37) are precedent agent-workflow-only rows; design AC4/AC8 correctly add an agent-workflow-only row for /adv-coordinate.

sources.label: adv-cleanup command skeleton (cited model)
sources.summary: Report-first, dry-run-default, approval-gated active-state triage workflow with manifest comment, Command Boundary, phased reads. Sound boring precedent for /adv-coordinate's read-first-then-approval-gated structure.

sources.label: approved ChangeContract (agreement-derived)
sources.summary: Standard-rigor contract with 11 ACs, 4 constraints, 6 avoidances. Design decisions D1-D6 and design-derived criteria map 1:1 onto every contract item; no contract item is unaddressed.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is a boring, additive, spec-aligned extension. It introduces NO new MCP tool, Temporal workflow, persistent coordination state, or CLI mutation verb — it composes existing typed Epic tools (adv_epic_list/show for reads; adv_epic_update/reorder/repair_membership for approval-gated writes) behind a Markdown command contract, exactly mirroring the established adv-cleanup and adv-epic patterns. The reuse of existing structural safeguards (expected_version optimistic concurrency for update/reorder; evidence+dryRun for membership repair; target-path trust) means correctness stays machine-checkable rather than prose-only, satisfying P33. Internal consistency is sound: the 27→28 command count, the required 'Audit' strong-verb allowlist addition, the agent-workflow-only CLI classification, and the rq-epicCoordinateCommand01 spec requirement are all individually verified against current source. No simpler viable approach exists that still delivers a first-class command surface — a pure ad-hoc agent instruction would skip the manifest/doc/test/spec gates the codebase enforces for every /adv-* command. No spec contradiction found; the advisory-order and optional-membership invariants are explicitly preserved.

validation.notes: All four validation dimensions clear. CORRECTNESS: design addresses every contract item AC1-AC11/C1-C4/DONT1-DONT6 with no logical gap; the only verification-sensitive numeric (command count 28) is confirmed as current-27+1. SIMPLICITY: this IS the boring path — no new runtime primitive; composes existing typed tools. SPEC-LAW: aligns with advance-epics rq-epicOrderAdvisory01/OptionalMembership01/CreateCommand01/MembershipRepair01/MutableScope01; no contradiction. ALTERNATIVES: an aggregate coordination MCP tool was correctly rejected in discovery (C1) as over-scoped with no structural gap forcing it. Minor advisory (non-blocking): implementers must add 'Audit' to STRONG_VERBS in manifest.test.ts in the same change or the strong-verb test fails — design D2 already calls this out, so it is covered, not a defect.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED — proceed to planning. The design is correct, materially simple (no new tool/workflow/state/CLI verb), and spec-compliant. It faithfully reuses the adv-cleanup report-first/approval-gated skeleton and the existing typed Epic tool safeguards (expected_version, evidence, dryRun, target trust). Every approved contract item maps to a concrete design decision. Two execution-time reminders already embedded in the design and worth re-confirming during prep: (1) add 'Audit' to the STRONG_VERBS allowlist in manifest.test.ts (line 188-210) alongside the manifest entry, since the description verb is otherwise rejected; (2) bump the manifest length assertion at manifest.test.ts:64 from 27 to 28. Both are anticipated by AC2/AC3/D2 — no design change required.
follow_ups: At prep, ensure the red-test task explicitly covers the STRONG_VERBS allowlist addition ('Audit') and the manifest length 27→28 bump so AC2/AC3 fail-first before implementation.
Confirm cli-surface-matrix.test.ts is included in the targeted verification command (design already lists it) so the new agent-workflow-only row is enforced (AC8).

nonblocking_findings.label: praise

nonblocking_findings.why: rq-epicCoordinateCommand01 covers read-first reporting, advisory order, approval-gated typed mutation, and forbids mandatory membership, auto-enrollment, Jira-like workflow, direct state edits, and CLI mutation verbs.

wisdom.content: Manifest command count was already stale relative to wording: before adding adv-coordinate, COMMAND_MANIFEST had 28 entries while manifest.test.ts label/count expected 27. When adding a command, verify actual Object.keys(COMMAND_MANIFEST).length before hardcoding count-based AC/tests; be ready to correct approved criteria if source evidence contradicts discovery assumptions.

items.text: **Command asset exists:** `.opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md` exists with frontmatter name `adv-coordinate`, manifest comment, Command Boundary, read-first phases, report output, and approval-gated apply phase.

**Manifest registered:** `plugin/src/manifest.ts` contains `adv-coordinate` with description `Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health`, `requiresChangeId: false`, no gate, and no `phaseGoal`.

**No direct state edits:** Tests or static checks prove the command does not instruct direct ADV state-file reads/edits.
**No CLI mutation surface:** CLI-surface docs classify `/adv-coordinate` as `agent-workflow-only`, and no `bin/adv` mutation command is added.
**Advisory order preserved:** Command text and tests state Epic order recommendations never block gates, tasks, promotion, or change progress.
**Optional membership preserved:** Command text and tests state Epic membership remains optional and no auto-enrollment occurs.

Reuse existing typed Epic tools; do not add a new aggregate coordination MCP tool unless a structural gap is proven.
Durable Epic mutation requires explicit approval, current `expected_version` when required, and audit evidence where tool contracts require it.
Product-spanning and target-path behavior must follow existing target trust rules.
Keep command prose terse and consistent with `docs/command-voice-standard.md`.
No mandatory Epic membership.
No auto-enrollment of changes into Epics.
No hard-blocking based on Epic order.
No Jira-like assignments, estimates, sprints, boards, or ownership workflow.
No CLI mutation verbs for coordination.
No silent durable edits.

documents.proposal:
## Cross-Project Origin

This change was created as a follow-up from **pokeedge**.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Source project | pokeedge |
| Source path | `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` |

> Origin context: pokeedge surfaced a need for ADV to coordinate multiple Epics as a set after a real Epic alignment/sequencing pass.

# Add `/adv-coordinate` command

## Why

ADV lacks a first-class coordination workflow across active Epics. Multi-Epic projects can drift: narratives go stale, work can be tracked in the wrong Epic, cross-Epic prerequisites stay implicit, advisory order can stop matching dependencies, and membership projections can become unhealthy. Today ADV handles this ad hoc. The desired outcome is a read-first `/adv-coordinate` command that inventories Epics, reports alignment/sequencing/health findings, and applies durable Epic updates only after explicit user approval.

## What Changes

Add `/adv-coordinate` as an ADV command entry point for Epic-set coordination. The command should instruct ADV to:

- inventory active Epics and their entries through typed Epic/change tools;
- audit ownership boundaries, narrative accuracy, cross-Epic dependencies, advisory sequence, capstone placement, and membership health;
- separate evidence-backed findings from judgment calls;
- present a coordination report before any durable mutation;
- apply Epic narrative updates, reorders, and membership repairs only after user approval through typed tools.

## User Outcomes

- Users can ask ADV for one coordination pass across all active Epics instead of relying on ad hoc reasoning.
- Users see which Epic owns each piece of work and where cross-Epic dependencies should be made explicit.
- Users get recommended advisory reorders when current Epic order conflicts with prerequisite/capstone flow.
- Users can approve or reject durable Epic edits with the rationale visible before state changes.
- Users get health guidance for stale or unreachable Epic membership projections without hidden mutations.

## Scope

### In Scope

- New `.opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md` command contract.
- `plugin/src/manifest.ts` command registration for `adv-coordinate`.
- Manifest/doc drift updates where command tables or command-count tests require the new command.
- Command-suite documentation updates in `README.md`, `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `SETUP.md`, and `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md` where required by existing tests or docs consistency.
- Spec-law update or amendment if discovery determines a durable command-suite or Epic workflow requirement is missing.
- Tests that prove command asset, manifest, and documentation consistency for the new command.

### Out of Scope

- Creating new Epic mutation tools.
- Making Epic membership mandatory.
- Auto-enrolling existing changes into Epics.
- Treating Epic order as a hard gate or blocker.
- Implementing CLI mutation verbs for Epic coordination.
- Running an actual coordination pass over this or another product as part of this change.

### Must Not

- Must not directly read or edit ADV state files.
- Must not silently mutate Epic narratives, entry order, or membership projection state.
- Must not present heuristic ownership/sequencing conclusions as authoritative when typed/source evidence is available.
- Must not collapse distinct Epic work solely for cosmetic simplicity.
- Must not add Jira-like assignments, estimates, sprints, boards, or ownership workflow.

## Affected Code

Quick checks identified likely affected surfaces:

- `.opencode/command/` — existing command contracts live here; `adv-epic.md` and `adv-cleanup.md` show nearby read-first/approval-gated patterns.
- `plugin/src/manifest.ts` — type-safe command manifest; new command needs phase, scope, prerequisites, and successors.
- `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts` — explicit command list/count currently covers 27 commands and will need update if a command is added; strong-verb checks constrain the manifest description.
- `plugin/src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` — requires every command doc to have a manifest entry and matching descriptions in docs tables.
- `README.md`, `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `SETUP.md`, and `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md` — command reference and CLI-surface documentation should be updated for consistency.
- `plugin/src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts` or a neighboring asset test — likely place to assert the new coordination command contract if a spec delta is added.

## Related Repositories

- Current implementation target: `advance`.
- Originating context: `pokeedge` follow-up. No code mutation expected in `pokeedge` for this change.

## Constraints

- Specs remain law; any durable Epic/command behavior not already specified must be handled as a spec delta during discovery/design.
- Epic order remains advisory only.
- Epic membership remains optional.
- Product-spanning Epic state must respect typed target-path trust rules.
- Durable mutations in the command workflow require explicit approval and typed tools.
- Structural correctness (P33) applies: typed reads/tools own correctness; heuristics may rank and triage only.

## Impact

- Gives users a named workflow for cross-Epic coordination.
- Reduces repeated ad hoc agent behavior for alignment/sequencing reviews.
- Increases command-suite surface area by one command and requires manifest/doc/test consistency.
- Likely requires adding a small `advance-epics` requirement around Epic coordination so avoidances and approval gates become spec law.

## Context

Relevant existing spec context:

- `advance-epics` defines typed Epic records, optional membership, advisory order, membership repair, scope metadata, and typed Epic tools.
- `advance-workflow` defines the seven-gate workflow, approval boundaries, and command/gate behavior.
- `advance-meta` includes command bridge and diagnostics patterns that constrain command-suite behavior.

Relevant existing code/test context:

- Command docs are Markdown files under `.opencode/command/`.
- `COMMAND_MANIFEST` is the canonical command registry.
- Drift tests require manifest entries, command docs, frontmatter descriptions, and command tables to stay synchronized.

## Research Validation

>>> SYNTHESIS COMPLETE <<<

# Architecture Research: `adv-coordinate`

## Summary

Validation result: **VALIDATED**. Adding `/adv-coordinate` as a read-first command document plus manifest/docs/tests is architecturally sound. It should not be a new MCP tool and should not add CLI mutation verbs. Existing typed Epic tools already provide the durable mutation surface; the command should orchestrate reads, report findings, and require approval before invoking those tools.

## Architecture Health Assessment

### Classification: SOUND

| Area | Existing | Reference | Deviation | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Command entry point | Markdown prompt in `.opencode/command/` plus `COMMAND_MANIFEST` entry | Existing `/adv-cleanup` and `/adv-epic` command patterns | NONE | Low-risk addition if drift surfaces updated |
| Mutation authority | Typed Epic tools own updates/reorders/repairs | `adv_epic_update`, `adv_epic_reorder`, `adv_epic_repair_membership` | NONE | No new mutation tool needed |
| Approval model | Durable state changes require explicit approval/audit evidence | `/adv-cleanup` Tier B and Epic tool audit/expected-version contracts | NONE | Command must be report-first and approval-gated |
| Epic semantics | Optional membership, advisory order, typed repairs | `advance-epics` spec law | NONE | Avoidances align with spec law |
| Docs/tests | Manifest is single source of truth; drift tests enforce tables | `manifest-doc-drift.test.ts`, `manifest.test.ts` | MINOR docs/count work | Must update all command tables and count tests |

## Validated Decisions

- Use command workflow, not MCP tool: existing command architecture is the right layer for agent judgment, HITL, and multi-step synthesis.
- Reuse existing Epic tools: narrative update, reorder, and repair already have typed tool contracts, expected-version/audit/dry-run safeguards.
- Model workflow on `/adv-cleanup`: dry-run/report-first, command boundary, explicit mutation approval, no hidden state edits.
- Keep `adv-coordinate` non-gate and non-workflow in manifest: no `gate`, no `phaseGoal`, no task graph ownership.
- Classify as `agent-workflow-only` in CLI-surface docs: coordination requires agent judgment and HITL approval.

## Simplification Opportunities

| Current | Simpler Alternative | Effort | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Build aggregate coordination MCP tool | Markdown command orchestrating existing typed tools | Low | Use command only |
| Add CLI mutation verb | Keep CLI read-only/status-like surfaces separate | Low | Do not add CLI mutation |
| Add new approval mechanism | Reuse existing Tier B inline / typed audit patterns | Low | Reuse existing approval voice |
| Treat ordering as enforcement | Keep advisory reorder recommendations | Low | Warn/recommend only |

## Concerns

- Manifest description must start with an allowed strong verb. `Coordinate ...` will fail current `manifest.test.ts`; use e.g. `Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health` unless discovery chooses to extend the verb allowlist.
- `manifest.test.ts` hardcodes 27 commands. Adding this command requires updating expected list and count to 28.
- Documentation drift surfaces extend beyond initial proposal: `SETUP.md` and `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md` should be included alongside `README.md` and `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`.
- A small `advance-epics` spec delta is recommended so coordination avoidances are capability law, not prompt-only convention.

## Anti-Patterns Detected

None in proposed architecture. Anti-patterns to avoid during design:

- New aggregate tool that duplicates existing Epic tool authority.
- Silent durable edits after report generation.
- Hard-blocking changes based on Epic order.
- Mandatory Epic enrollment.
- CLI mutation path for coordination.

## Over-Engineering Flags

- New workflow state for coordination is unnecessary in v1.
- New schema for dependency graph may be premature; command can derive report structure from current Epic entries and typed tool outputs.
- Cross-product source probing should stay bounded and trust-rule aware; do not build broad project crawler behavior into v1.

## Action Items

1. Add `adv-coordinate` manifest entry with allowed strong-verb description, likely `Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health`.
2. Add `.opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md`, modeled on `adv-cleanup.md` + `adv-epic.md`.
3. Update `manifest.test.ts` expected command list/count.
4. Update `README.md`, `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `SETUP.md`, and `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md` command tables/classification.
5. Add or extend asset tests for command contract: read-first inventory, alignment/sequencing/health report, approval-gated `adv_epic_update`/`adv_epic_reorder`/`adv_epic_repair_membership`, advisory order, optional membership, no direct ADV state reads.
6. Add `advance-epics` spec requirement if discovery confirms coordination is durable capability law.

## Confidence

High: existing repo architecture has clear command/manifest/drift-test pattern, and Epic tools/specs already cover the durable mutation boundary.

Medium: exact user-facing approval grammar and dry-run/execute flags should be finalized in discovery/design against current command voice rules.

## Sources

- `plugin/src/manifest.ts` — `CommandDef` shape and command registry.
- `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts` — 27-command count/list and strong-verb description tests.
- `plugin/src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` — manifest/doc/frontmatter/table drift enforcement.
- `docs/command-voice-standard.md` — manifest description and command-doc contract.
- `.opencode/command/adv-cleanup.md` — report-first, approval-gated command pattern.
- `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` — typed Epic creation/update command pattern.
- `plugin/src/tools/epic.ts` — typed Epic read/update/reorder/repair tools.
- `plugin/src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts` — Epic command/spec instruction asset tests.
- `advance-epics` spec via `adv_spec` — optional membership, advisory order, typed/audited repairs.
- OpenCode command docs: `https://opencode.ai/docs/commands` — Markdown commands, frontmatter, `$ARGUMENTS`.

## Discovery Agenda

Codebase unknowns to resolve in discovery:

- Exact manifest phase/scope for `adv-coordinate`: likely `advanced`, reads over `epics`, `changes`, and `specs`; modifies should likely be empty unless existing command definitions require listing possible typed-tool mutations.
- Whether to update strong-verb allowlist or choose existing verb; research recommends choosing existing verb.
- Whether a new command-specific asset test is warranted or `advance-epics-assets.test.ts` should be extended.
- Whether `docs/command-voice-standard.md` needs any command-specific approval guidance; research suggests existing approval voice is enough.

Spec unknowns to resolve in discovery:

- Exact text for a new `advance-epics` requirement, likely `rq-epicCoordinateCommand01`.
- Whether command coordination belongs only in `advance-epics`, or also needs a cross-link from `advance-workflow`.

Domain/user unknowns to resolve in discovery:

- Default breadth: all active Epics by default, with optional filters later, or filtered command arguments in v1.
- Whether v1 should expose `--dry-run` / `--execute` flags, or remain report-first with inline approval only.

Integration unknowns to resolve in discovery:

- Exact target-path wording for product-spanning Epics when targets are unreachable.
- Evidence citation format for cross-project findings without leaking untrusted target details.

# Agreement — Add `/adv-coordinate` command

## Lineage

- Cross-project origin: `pokeedge` at `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge`.
- Source path validated as an existing project checkout.
- Source change ID: not provided in origin metadata.

## Correction

During apply, the current manifest was found to already contain 28 commands before adding `adv-coordinate`. User approved correcting the count criterion from 28 to 29 via question tool: `Approve correction`.

## Objective

Add a first-class `/adv-coordinate` command that audits active Epics as a set, reports alignment/sequencing/membership-health findings, and applies durable Epic updates only through existing typed tools after explicit user approval.

## Discovery Findings

- Research validation: `SOUND` / `VALIDATED`.
- Main finding: `/adv-coordinate` should be a Markdown command + manifest/docs/tests change, not a new MCP tool or CLI mutation verb.
- Active change scan found no direct owner conflict.
- `adv_change_validate` passed pre-prep with expected warnings only.
- Adjacent agenda items (`Fix Epic cross-project guidance`, `Fix Epic tool warrant surface`) should inform wording but do not block.
- Best pattern: model on `/adv-cleanup` read-first/report-first/approval-gated flow and `/adv-epic` typed Epic mutation boundaries.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Command asset exists:** `.opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md` exists with frontmatter name `adv-coordinate`, manifest comment, Command Boundary, read-first phases, report output, and approval-gated apply phase.
2. **Manifest registered:** `plugin/src/manifest.ts` contains `adv-coordinate` with description `Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health`, `requiresChangeId: false`, no gate, and no `phaseGoal`.
3. **Command count updated:** `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts` expected command list includes `adv-coordinate` and count is 29.
4. **Docs synchronized:** README, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS, SETUP, and `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md` contain the new command with matching description/classification where applicable.
5. **Spec law updated:** `advance-epics` includes `rq-epicCoordinateCommand01` covering read-first report, advisory order, approval-gated typed mutation, and no new planning primitive.
6. **Asset tests prove command contract:** Tests assert `/adv-coordinate` uses `adv_epic_list`/`adv_epic_show`, reports ownership/narrative/dependency/sequencing/health findings, and references `adv_epic_update`, `adv_epic_reorder`, and `adv_epic_repair_membership` only as approval-gated actions.
7. **No direct state edits:** Tests or static checks prove the command does not instruct direct ADV state-file reads/edits.
8. **No CLI mutation surface:** CLI-surface docs classify `/adv-coordinate` as `agent-workflow-only`, and no `bin/adv` mutation command is added.
9. **Advisory order preserved:** Command text and tests state Epic order recommendations never block gates, tasks, promotion, or change progress.
10. **Optional membership preserved:** Command text and tests state Epic membership remains optional and no auto-enrollment occurs.
11. **Verification passes:** Targeted tests covering manifest/doc drift and Epic command assets pass through `bin/oc-test targeted -- ...` or equivalent repo-approved test route.

## Constraints

- Reuse existing typed Epic tools; do not add a new aggregate coordination MCP tool unless a structural gap is proven.
- Durable Epic mutation requires explicit approval, current `expected_version` when required, and audit evidence where tool contracts require it.
- Product-spanning and target-path behavior must follow existing target trust rules.
- Keep command prose terse and consistent with `docs/command-voice-standard.md`.

## Avoidances

- No mandatory Epic membership.
- No auto-enrollment of changes into Epics.
- No hard-blocking based on Epic order.
- No Jira-like assignments, estimates, sprints, boards, or ownership workflow.
- No CLI mutation verbs for coordination.
- No silent durable edits.

documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview

Add `/adv-coordinate` as an agent-workflow command over existing Epic tools. The command remains a Markdown workflow contract under `.opencode/command/`, registered in `COMMAND_MANIFEST`, documented in command tables, and covered by asset/drift tests. It does not add a new MCP tool, Temporal workflow, persistent coordination state, or CLI mutation verb.

Runtime behavior is read-first:

1. Inventory active Epics with `adv_epic_list` and `adv_epic_show view:"full"`.
2. Analyze alignment, narrative accuracy, cross-Epic dependencies, advisory sequencing, capstone placement, and membership health.
3. Present a coordination report.
4. Apply durable Epic actions only after explicit approval, using existing typed tools.

Durable actions reuse existing structural safeguards:

- `adv_epic_update` for title/narrative updates with `expected_version`.
- `adv_epic_reorder` for advisory order changes with `expected_version`.
- `adv_epic_repair_membership` for projection repair with `evidence`, `dryRun`, and target-path trust support.

## Key Decisions

### D1 — Command workflow, not new tool

Use `.opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md` plus manifest/docs/tests. Coordination involves judgment, report synthesis, and HITL approval, matching existing command workflows such as `/adv-cleanup` and `/adv-epic`. A new MCP tool would duplicate authority already owned by typed Epic tools.

### D2 — Manifest entry shape

Add `adv-coordinate` to `plugin/src/manifest.ts`:

- `phase: "advanced"`
- `requiresChangeId: false`
- `prerequisites: []`
- `successors: []`
- no `gate`
- no `phaseGoal`
- scope reads `specs`, `epics`, and `changes`; modifies remains empty because durable mutation authority is approval-gated through typed tools.

Description: `Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health`.

Add `Audit` to the strong-verb allowlist in `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts`.

### D3 — Command contract skeleton

Model `.opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md` on `/adv-cleanup`:

- Command Boundary: produces report + approved action results; Gate: None.
- Default mode: read-only/report-first.
- Phases: parse arguments, inventory, alignment audit, sequencing audit, health audit, present report, approval, apply approved actions.
- Durable actions require explicit approval; no LLM fallback for mutation approval.
- Stale `expected_version` forces re-read and re-present.

### D4 — Spec-law update

Add `rq-epicCoordinateCommand01` to `.adv/specs/advance-epics/spec.json` and mirror in `docs/specs/advance-epics.md`. Bump `advance-epics` version from `1.4.0` to `1.5.0`.

### D5 — Test strategy

Use existing structural tests:

- `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts`: add `adv-coordinate`, update count to 29, add `Audit` strong verb.
- `plugin/src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts`: existing command/doc/table drift coverage.
- `plugin/src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts`: add `adv-coordinate` as `agent-workflow-only`.
- `plugin/src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts`: assert spec ID and command contract snippets.

## Affected Components

- `.opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md`
- `plugin/src/manifest.ts`
- `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts`
- `plugin/src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts`
- `.adv/specs/advance-epics/spec.json`
- `docs/specs/advance-epics.md`
- `README.md`
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`
- `SETUP.md`
- `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md`

## Design-Derived Criteria

- Command manifest description must be exactly `Audit Epic alignment, sequencing, and membership health` across manifest, command frontmatter, README, and ADV_INSTRUCTIONS drift-tested tables.
- `Audit` must be accepted by manifest description tests.
- The command must be `agent-workflow-only` in CLI matrix.
- Spec mirror and JSON spec must both include `rq-epicCoordinateCommand01`.
- Targeted verification must include `src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts`.
- Command contract must instruct re-read/re-present on stale `expected_version`.

## Risks / Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Manifest/doc drift red CI | Update manifest, command frontmatter, README, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS together; rely on drift test. |
| Missing CLI matrix row | Include `src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts` and add row. |
| Spec/document mirror drift | Update JSON spec, docs mirror, and asset tests together. |
| Hidden mutation semantics | Command text says report-first and typed tools only; tests assert typed tool names and no direct state edits. |
| Over-scoped coordination engine | Keep v1 as command prompt over existing tools; no new persistent graph/state. |

## Independent Design Validation

Validator result: `VALIDATED`.

Correction: apply-time evidence changed command count criterion from 28 to 29. User approved correction. Design remains otherwise unchanged.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome

Delivered a read-first `/adv-coordinate` workflow command for Epic coordination. The change adds command, manifest, docs, spec-law, and test coverage without adding new MCP tools, Temporal workflows, persistent coordination state, or CLI mutation verbs.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Added RED contract tests for `/adv-coordinate` manifest/spec/command surfaces and captured expected failing evidence before implementation.
2. Updated `advance-epics` spec law with `rq-epicCoordinateCommand01`, version `1.5.0`, and docs mirror coverage.
3. Added `.opencode/command/adv-coordinate.md` with read-first Epic inventory/audit/report flow and approval-gated typed action phase.
4. Registered `adv-coordinate` in `COMMAND_MANIFEST`, synchronized README/ADV_INSTRUCTIONS/SETUP/CLI matrix docs, and classified it as `agent-workflow-only`.
5. Ran final targeted verification and closed the AC6 test gap found during review.

## What Was Verified

- Verdict: APPROVED / READY. Acceptance review found 0 blockers and 0 issues; reviewer added scoped AC6 asset assertions and reran verification.
- Tests: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/manifest.test.ts src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts` passed: 4 files, 110 tests.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — change is command/docs/spec/test workflow work, with no frontend, browser-visible, or visual-output surface.
- Contract matrix: 21 required rows persisted; 11 acceptance criteria passed; 4 constraints respected; 6 avoidances respected; 0 fail/violated/unknown rows.

## Remaining Concerns

- Full `pnpm run check` not rerun in acceptance; targeted suite passed and matches agreed verification scope.

Spec task added `rq-epicCoordinateCommand01` to `.adv/specs/advance-epics/spec.json`, mirrored in `docs/specs/advance-epics.md`, and JSON parse/static check passed. |

Reviewer fixed scoped test gap by adding explicit AC6 assertions for ownership, narrative, dependency, sequencing/health/capstone report dimensions and approval-gated typed action references. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | **No direct state edits:** Tests or static checks prove the command does not instruct direct ADV state-file reads/edits. | pass | Targeted verification passed (110 tests). Asset tests assert command does not instruct direct ADV state-file reads/edits; review found no direct state-file access guidance. |

CLI-surface matrix classifies `/adv-coordinate` as `agent-workflow-only`; no `bin/adv` mutation verb was added. |
| AC9 | acceptance_criterion | **Advisory order preserved:** Command text and tests state Epic order recommendations never block gates, tasks, promotion, or change progress. | pass | Targeted verification passed (110 tests). Command text and asset tests state Epic order recommendations are advisory and never block gates, tasks, promotion, or change progress. |
| AC10 | acceptance_criterion | **Optional membership preserved:** Command text and tests state Epic membership remains optional and no auto-enrollment occurs. | pass | Targeted verification passed (110 tests). Command text and asset tests state Epic membership remains optional and no auto-enrollment occurs. |

Reviewer reran same suite after adding AC6 assertions; still passed. |
| C1 | constraint | Reuse existing typed Epic tools; do not add a new aggregate coordination MCP tool unless a structural gap is proven. | respected | Review verified implementation is command asset + manifest/docs/tests only. No new aggregate MCP tool, Temporal workflow, or persistent coordination state added; durable actions reuse `adv_epic_*` tools. |
| C2 | constraint | Durable Epic mutation requires explicit approval, current `expected_version` when required, and audit evidence where tool contracts require it. | respected | Command contract requires explicit approval before durable actions, uses `expected_version` for update/reorder, and includes evidence requirements for typed repair/update operations. Asset tests assert approval-gated action references. |
| C3 | constraint | Product-spanning and target-path behavior must follow existing target trust rules. | respected | Command contract preserves target-path trust by routing cross-project/product-spanning actions through existing typed Epic tools and their target confirmation/evidence rules; no bypass path introduced. |
| C4 | constraint | Keep command prose terse and consistent with `docs/command-voice-standard.md`. | respected | Docs and command prose updated in terse command style; manifest/doc drift coverage passed. Reviewer reported READY with no blocking prose/style findings. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | No mandatory Epic membership. | respected | Reviewer verdict READY. Command text/tests state Epic membership remains optional; no mandatory membership language added. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | No auto-enrollment of changes into Epics. | respected | Reviewer verdict READY. Command text/tests state no auto-enrollment; no tool or workflow was added to auto-link changes to Epics. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | No hard-blocking based on Epic order. | respected | Reviewer verdict READY. Command text/tests state advisory order recommendations never block gates, tasks, promotion, or change progress. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | No Jira-like assignments, estimates, sprints, boards, or ownership workflow. | respected | Reviewer verdict READY. Command adds Epic coordination report/action workflow only; no assignments, estimates, sprints, boards, or ownership workflow were added. |
| DONT5 | avoidance | No CLI mutation verbs for coordination. | respected | Reviewer verdict READY and CLI matrix test passed. `/adv-coordinate` is `agent-workflow-only`; no `bin/adv` mutation command added. |
| DONT6 | avoidance | No silent durable edits. | respected | Reviewer verdict READY. Command requires explicit approval before durable edits and routes them through typed tools with evidence/version fields; no silent durable edit path introduced. |

clarify_findings.message: Proposal has no Scope section. What files and modules will be affected?
Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
